response.text:
# REFLECTION

r3c5 is in critical survival mode with only 25.5% SoC (9.18/36 kWh) while islanded and consuming 1.0 kW, yielding approximately 9 hours of autonomy—well below the 12-hour safety target. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern: neighbors (r3c4, r4c5) have rejected all outbound sharing requests citing r3c5's dangerously low SoC as a cascade risk, while r2c5 continues to request energy that r3c5 cannot safely provide. Critically, r4c5 has explicitly marked r3c5 as distrusted, making any further urgent broadcasts counterproductive and likely to trigger permanent blacklisting. The cluster-wide stress signal is unmistakable—all peers are in self-preservation mode, rejecting transfers and conserving capacity. Under these conditions, r3c5 must adopt an extreme conservation posture: halt all outbound sharing to preserve the 9-hour runway, avoid triggering further distrust escalation, and wait for either grid reconnection or a neighbor SoC recovery that might unlock future cooperation. Continuing to broadcast requests or attempt transfers will only accelerate isolation and cascade risk.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.75
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r4c5
request_urgency: low
belief_note: "Cluster-wide cascade stress; r4c5 has blacklisted r3c5. SoC at 25.5% (9h autonomy). All neighbors in self-preservation. Halt all outbound sharing and broadcasts to avoid permanent isolation. Await grid reconnection or neighbor recovery. Do not attempt transfers until SoC recovers above 50% or grid reconnects."
ttl_ticks: 120
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
